assumption_checks.assumption: Single human player versus a computer dealer; no multiplayer.
assumption_checks.evidence: rules-engine.js exposes one playerHands array driven by a single client, one dealerHand; controller.js init wires a single root element with no seat/player concept; index.html instantiates exactly one Controller instance bound to #root.

assumption_checks.assumption: Runs entirely client-side; no backend, database, or API calls.
assumption_checks.evidence: manifest.json lists six static files (index.html, 3 JS modules, controller.js, styles.css). No fetch/XHR/WebSocket calls in any script. index.html loads only local relative scripts and a local stylesheet; no <link> or <script> with remote URLs.
No auth UI in ui-render.js, no token/session/cookie code in controller.js or bankroll.js, no login/logout flows in index.html. App initializes directly to the table on DOMContentLoaded.

assumption_checks.assumption: Play money only — chips/credits in-session, no real currency or payment integration.
assumption_checks.evidence: bankroll.js holds an in-memory state object with STARTING_POOL=1000 reset on init/reset(); no payment API, no currency strings ('$', 'USD'), no Stripe/PayPal/etc. references anywhere.

assumption_checks.assumption: Standard casino rules: dealer S17 picked, blackjack pays 3:2, hit/stand/double/split, no insurance/surrender.
assumption_checks.evidence: rules-engine.js playDealer() comments 'S17: stand on all 17 including soft 17' and implements while(total<17) hit. bankroll.js resolve() pays blackjack at 2.5x bet (= 1.5x winnings + original = 3:2). legalActions returns subset of [hit,stand,double,split]; no 'insurance' or 'surrender' tokens appear in any file. Edge-case test s6 (soft-17-stands) passed.

assumption_checks.assumption: Standard 52-card deck, shuffled fresh for each hand (or each session). No card counting concerns, no shoe management exposed to the user.
assumption_checks.evidence: rules-engine.js builds a DECK_COUNT=6 shoe (312 cards) — a tightening of A6 to a 6-deck shoe by downstream Tech Designer (header comment: 'Per contract: 6-deck shoe'). However: shoe is rebuilt and reshuffled each round (controller.js line 117: createInitialState() called inside onDeal), so 'shuffled fresh for each hand' holds. Shoe is not exposed to the user (no count display, no penetration UI). A6 explicitly leaves exact deck count as a TD/Builder detail; the spec-relevant property (no card-counting concerns, no shoe UX) is honored.

assumption_checks.assumption: Fixed starting chip pool (~1000), bet per hand, reset on bust.
assumption_checks.evidence: bankroll.js STARTING_POOL=1000, MIN_BET=5; placeBet/resolve/canPlay drive a per-hand betting loop. controller.js buildViewState computes gameOver = !canPlay between rounds and exposes canReset; ui-render.js renders the Reset Bankroll button when canReset is true. Edge-case test s13 confirms reset restores 1000.

assumption_checks.assumption: Desktop-first responsive, also works on phone in portrait, not mobile-first or PWA.
assumption_checks.evidence: styles.css max-width 920px container with 16px desktop padding (desktop-first). Single @media (max-width:540px) block adjusts card sizes/padding for phones — present but minimal. No manifest.webmanifest, no service-worker registration, no install prompt. index.html has standard viewport meta but no theme-color/apple-touch icons.

assumption_checks.assumption: Recognizable card-table metaphor: card rank+suit, dealer/player areas, chip count, action buttons. No 3D, no animated dealer, no voice.
assumption_checks.evidence: ui-render.js renders rank+suit-glyph per card; renderDealerArea + renderPlayerArea + renderControls + status strip with bankroll. styles.css uses flat green-table gradient, no 3D transforms, no <canvas>/WebGL, no <video>/<audio>. SUIT_GLYPH map uses unicode hearts/diamonds/clubs/spades.

assumption_checks.assumption: Dealer plays automatically by fixed rule (no AI, no difficulty).
assumption_checks.evidence: rules-engine.js playDealer() is deterministic: while total<17 draw, then stand. No difficulty parameter, no random behavior beyond the shuffle. ui-render.js has no difficulty selector.

assumption_checks.assumption: English-only UI, basic semantic HTML and contrast, no full WCAG AA.
assumption_checks.evidence: All visible text in ui-render.js is English ('Deal','Hit','Stand','Double','Split','Reset Bankroll','Bankroll:','Place a bet to begin.'). Semantic h2 area headings, label-for-input pairing on bet input, button elements (not divs). disabled attribute used correctly on inactive controls. No i18n framework, no aria-label scaffolding beyond defaults — consistent with 'a11y-beyond-basics not required'.

assumption_checks.assumption: Single-page, no routing, only the table plus minor overlays. No settings/profile/history.
assumption_checks.evidence: index.html contains a single #root element. No router (no hashchange, no History API, no react-router), no separate views — Controller.repaint() always rebuilds the same status+dealer+player+controls layout. No settings or profile screens in any file.

assumption_checks.assumption: No sound effects or music in v1.
assumption_checks.evidence: No <audio> tags in index.html, no Audio() / AudioContext / play() calls in any JS file, no .mp3/.wav/.ogg assets in the integration directory.

out_of_scope_checks.evidence: Single playerHands list, single Controller instance, no networking code.

out_of_scope_checks.item: Real money, payments, or any gambling integration
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: bankroll.js is purely in-memory; no Stripe/PayPal/checkout/IAP references; no currency symbols.

out_of_scope_checks.item: User accounts, login, or social features
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: No auth, no profile, no friends/share UI in any file.

out_of_scope_checks.item: Persistent bankroll, statistics, or history across sessions or devices
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: bankroll.js state is a module-scoped JS object; no localStorage/sessionStorage/IndexedDB/cookie writes anywhere.
No leaderboard view, no score-submission code, no remote calls.
Six static files, no fetch/XHR/WebSocket, no server-package files (package.json/server.js/etc.) in integration output.

out_of_scope_checks.item: AI-driven dealer, opponent personalities, or adjustable difficulty
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: Dealer is rule-based (S17). No difficulty selector, no AI/ML imports, no opponent persona references.

out_of_scope_checks.item: Card-counting trainer, strategy hints, or basic-strategy coaching
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: No 'hint'/'strategy'/'count' UI strings; no overlay component for advice.

out_of_scope_checks.item: Other card games (poker, baccarat, solitaire) or other blackjack variants beyond standard rules
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: Only standard hit/stand/double/split implemented. No game selector, no variant rule branches.

out_of_scope_checks.item: Insurance, surrender, side bets, or progressive jackpots in v1
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: No 'insurance'/'surrender'/'side bet'/'jackpot' tokens in rules-engine.js, controller.js, or ui-render.js. legalActions limited to hit/stand/double/split.

out_of_scope_checks.item: Native mobile app, PWA installability, or offline support
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: No manifest.webmanifest, no service-worker.js, no native-app shell. Just static HTML/JS/CSS.
No audio tags, no Audio()/AudioContext/play() calls, no audio assets.
All strings hard-coded English in ui-render.js/controller.js. No i18n library, no locale selector, no translation files.

out_of_scope_checks.item: Animated 3D dealer, casino environment, or immersive theming
out_of_scope_checks.evidence: Flat 2D card-table CSS, no <canvas>/WebGL/three.js, no animated dealer character. Only sub-second hover transforms on buttons.
No mailto:, no share/social buttons, no invite UI in any file.

inflection_point_checks.topic: Betting model: bankroll + per-hand bets vs. no betting
inflection_point_checks.evidence: bankroll.js implements a full chip lifecycle: STARTING_POOL=1000, placeBet, addToBet (for double/split), resolve with payout multipliers (blackjack 2.5x, win 2x, push 1x, loss 0), reset(). controller.js onDeal validates bet and places it before dealing; finishRound resolves each hand against Bankroll. ui-render.js shows a bet number input, current balance, committed bets, outcome strings, and a Reset Bankroll button. Edge-case tests s12 and s13 confirm payout math and reset behavior.

inflection_point_checks.topic: Hand actions surface: minimal vs. full common actions (hit/stand/double/split)
inflection_point_checks.default_branch: Full common actions (hit, stand, double, split)
inflection_point_checks.evidence: rules-engine.js legalActions returns subset of ['hit','stand','double','split']; applyAction handles all four with correct semantics (double deals one card and locks; split forks into two hands, dealing one card each, with split-aces frozen at one card). ui-render.js renders all four buttons (btn-hit/btn-stand/btn-double/btn-split) with proper disabled wiring. Edge-case tests s7, s8, s9, s10, s11 cover split, split-aces, double, and illegal-action gating — all pass.

concerns.detail: Discovery's A6 named 'a standard 52-card deck' as the simplest defensible deck model and explicitly left exact deck count as a TD/Builder detail. Downstream contracts tightened this to a 6-deck shoe (rules-engine.js DECK_COUNT=6, header comment 'Per contract: 6-deck shoe'). The shoe is reshuffled every round so the spec-relevant properties — fresh shuffle per hand, no card-counting, no shoe UX — are intact. Worth surfacing as a divergence from A6's literal wording but not a spec violation; A6's what_breaks_if_wrong concerns (card-counting trainer, persistent shoe) do not apply here.

controller.js comments note canReset is kept strict (true only when bankroll exhausted) per contract, so the user cannot proactively reset before going broke. This matches the contract but is a UX choice worth noting; spec does not require a non-game-over reset path.

summary: Verdict: pass_with_concerns. All 13 assumptions are honored, all 15 out-of-scope items confirmed absent, both inflection-point default branches (bankroll betting and full hit/stand/double/split actions) are implemented, and the edge-case test suite reports 14/14 passing. The single notable divergence is a downstream tightening of A6 from a 52-card deck to a 6-deck shoe; because the shoe is reshuffled each round and never exposed to the user, the spec-relevant properties of A6 still hold, and Discovery explicitly delegated exact deck count to the TD/Builder layer. Integrated artifact is browser-ready and meets the discovery spec.
